summary: Mutation testing results: 30 killed, 9 timeout, 14 survived, 3 no coverage. Mutation score: 69.64% (below 80% threshold).
testQuality: Tests cover the happy path and primary error paths well, but the settled guard logic, response Content-Type header, server bind address, and server error path lack sufficient coverage to kill boundary mutants.

suggestions.description: Mutant survived: if (\!settled) changed to if (true) on line 49 — the double-callback guard is not verified
suggestions.rationale: No test sends a second valid token callback after the first. If the guard is removed, resolveToken and server.close() would be called twice, but no test catches this.
suggestions.suggestion: Add test: send two valid /callback?token= requests and assert tokenPromise resolves only once and the second response is still 200 (server was already closed) or that the resolved token value is from the first request only.

suggestions.description: Mutant survived: if (\!settled) changed to if (true) on line 57 — timeout guard not verified against already-settled server
suggestions.rationale: No test checks that if a token is received and then the timeout fires, rejectToken is not called a second time after the promise was already resolved.
suggestions.suggestion: Add test: start server with a short timeout, send valid token before timeout, await tokenPromise resolving, then await a small delay past the timeout and verify the resolved value is unchanged (no secondary rejection).

suggestions.description: Mutant survived: if (\!settled) changed to if (true) on line 66 — close() guard not verified against already-settled server via token path
suggestions.rationale: The close() test covers calling close() on an already-token-resolved server, but the settled flag mutation to false also survives, indicating the assertion does not distinguish between a no-op and an active rejection.
suggestions.suggestion: In the existing test startAuthServer close() can be called safely even after server already shut down, assert that after calling close() on an already-resolved server, tokenPromise still resolves with the original token value (not rejects).

suggestions.description: Mutant survived: Content-Type header object mutated to {} (line 46)
suggestions.rationale: No test inspects the Content-Type response header, so removing it goes undetected.
suggestions.suggestion: In the existing resolves with the token test, add: expect(response.headers.get(content-type)).toContain(text/plain).

suggestions.description: Mutant survived: server.listen bind address 127.0.0.1 mutated to  (line 77)
suggestions.rationale: No test verifies that the server binds only to loopback. Mutating the address to an empty string still allows the server to start and respond, so no test fails.
suggestions.suggestion: Add test: after startAuthServer(), verify that a fetch to http://localhost:{port}/callback (if different from 127.0.0.1 in the test environment) fails, or assert addr.address === 127.0.0.1 by exposing it on the handle or via a spy on server.listen.

suggestions.description: Mutant survived: server.on(error, reject) event name mutated to  (line 76)
suggestions.rationale: No test simulates a server listen error, so the error handler is never exercised.
suggestions.suggestion: Add test: mock http.createServer to return a server that emits an error event immediately on listen, and assert that startAuthServer() rejects with that error.

suggestions.description: Multiple mutants survived on the addr guard: if (false), logical && instead of ||, typeof check degraded (lines 79-80)
suggestions.rationale: The guard that rejects when addr is null or a string (Unix socket path) is never triggered by any test — all tests use a TCP server that returns an AddressInfo object.
suggestions.suggestion: Add test: mock server.address() to return null (or a string) after listen callback fires, and assert startAuthServer() rejects with Failed to get server address.
